content.text:
Checking how `gh extension install` resolves Go source repositories:

[REDACTED]

Having mapped the codebase: the app is a standalone Go CLI (`ghr`) with config under `~/.ghr` and env `GHR_CONFIG`. Converting it to a proper `gh` extension requires repo/binary naming, Cobra UX, state-directory migration, and release automation.

[REDACTED]

input.name: gh-wm extension migration
input.overview: Rename the project to `gh-wm` (GitHub repo `gh-wm`, Go module `github.com/an-lee/gh-wm`), ship a binary named `gh-wm` so the user-facing command is `gh wm`, rehome config to `~/.gh-wm` / `GH_WM_CONFIG`, and add standard gh extension release automation. Documentation, tests, and embedded strings (including remote Windows/Docker helpers) are updated consistently.
input.plan:
# gh-wm: full GitHub CLI extension migration

## What “gh extension” implies (constraints)

Per [GitHub’s extension docs](https://docs.github.com/en/github-cli/github-cli/creating-github-cli-extensions):

- **Repository name** must be `gh-<extension>` → **`gh-wm`** (command becomes **`gh wm`**).
- **Binary name** must match the repo: **`gh-wm`** (built from `cmd/gh-wm`).
- Arguments after `gh wm …` are passed **directly** to the extension binary (e.g. `gh wm status` → `os.Args` are like `gh-wm`, `status`, …). No code change needed beyond Cobra’s normal `Execute()` if `os.Args` is unchanged — gh invokes the extension executable with the tail args.

**Distribution:** Most users will install via **`gh extension install <owner>/gh-wm`**, which expects **release assets** with the `gh-wm-<os>-<arch>[.exe]` naming convention. Add the official [**gh-extension-precompile**](https://github.com/cli/gh-extension-precompile) workflow so tagged releases publish installable binaries.

**Manual / dev install:** `gh extension install .` from the repo root requires a built `gh-wm` binary at the expected location per gh’s extension rules — document `go build -o gh-wm ./cmd/gh-wm` then install locally.

---

## 1. Go module and package paths

- Change `go.mod` module path from `github.com/an-lee/ghr` to **`github.com/an-lee/gh-wm`**.
- Update **all** import paths (every file that imports `github.com/an-lee/ghr/...` — roughly 20+ Go files under `cmd/`, `internal/`).

---

## 2. CLI entrypoint and user-facing command strings

- Move **`cmd/ghr/main.go`** → **`cmd/gh-wm/main.go`**.
- Set the root Cobra command to **`Use: "wm"`** (matches extension name after the `gh-` prefix; help reads as `wm <subcommand>`; users invoke **`gh wm`**).
- Replace every user-visible **`ghr`** with **`gh wm`** in `Long`/`Short` text, errors, and `fmt` hints (large sweep across `main.go`, `internal/config/load.go`, `internal/tui/dashboard.go` `NonTTYHint`, `internal/runner/linux_sudo.go`, `internal/ops/ops.go`, `internal/doctor`, etc.).
- Update **`version`** subcommand description and any “quick setup” banners that say `ghr`.

---

## 3. Config directory and environment variables

Central definitions today live in [`internal/config/paths.go`](internal/config/paths.go):

- **`~/.ghr`** → **`~/.gh-wm`** (or `~/.gh-wm` consistently on Windows via existing `filepath` joins).
- **`GHR_CONFIG`** → **`GH_WM_CONFIG`** (document clearly; update [`internal/config/config_test.go`](internal/config/config_test.go) `ResolveConfigPath` tests).
- Rename **`GhrDir()`** to a clearer name (e.g. **`WmDir()`** or **`ConfigDir()`**) and update all callers (`main.go`, `mutate`, tests, runner paths).

**Migration for existing users:** In README/docs, add a short section: move `~/.ghr` → `~/.gh-wm`, set **`GH_WM_CONFIG`** if they used **`GHR_CONFIG`**. Optionally (if you want zero manual step), add a **one-time fallback** in `ResolveConfigPath` / home dir resolution: if the new path is missing but the old `~/.ghr` exists, use it and print a deprecation warning — this is a product choice; the minimal plan is doc-only migration.

Related strings/templates:

- [`internal/config/template.go`](internal/config/template.go), [`internal/config/runners.yml.template`](internal/config/runners.yml.template), [`internal/config/load.go`](internal/config/load.go) error hints.
- Remote **Windows** paths such as `USERPROFILE\.ghr\runners` in [`internal/runner/native.go`](internal/runner/native.go) / tests must become **`.gh-wm\runners`** where they refer to the tool’s config layout.
- **Docker on Windows** isolated config: rename PowerShell variables and temp dir from `ghr-docker-config` / `ghr.invalid` to neutral names (e.g. `gh-wm-docker-config` / `ghwm.invalid`) in [`internal/runner/docker.go`](internal/runner/docker.go) and [`internal/runner/docker_test.go`](internal/runner/docker_test.go).

**Note:** Docker **container** prefix `gh-runner-<name>` is unrelated to the CLI name; leave it unless you explicitly want a breaking rename for running containers.

---

## 4. Build tooling

- Update [`Makefile`](Makefile): **`BINARY := gh-wm`** (and `gh-wm.exe` on Windows), **`CMD_DIR := ./cmd/gh-wm`**.
- README install section: primary **`gh extension install <owner>/gh-wm`**; secondary **`go install github.com/an-lee/gh-wm/cmd/gh-wm@latest`** (binary on PATH is `gh-wm`; **`gh wm`** still requires extension install).

---

## 5. CI and releases

- Keep existing [`/.github/workflows/ci.yml`](.github/workflows/ci.yml) (vet + test).
- **Add** a release workflow using **`cli/gh-extension-precompile`** (trigger on tags `v*`), so `gh extension install` works for end users.
- Optional: add repository topic **`gh-extension`** on GitHub for discoverability (manual step in repo settings).

---

## 6. Documentation site (`docs/`)

Update [`docs/hugo.toml`](docs/hugo.toml): **`baseURL`**, **`title`**, **`BookRepo`**, **`BookEditPath`** to **`gh-wm`**. Sweep all Markdown under `docs/content/` and root [`README.md`](README.md): product name **GitHub Workflow Manager**, command examples **`gh wm`**, paths **`~/.gh-wm`**, env **`GH_WM_CONFIG`**.

[`/.github/workflows/pages.yml`](.github/workflows/pages.yml): after renaming the GitHub repo, Pages URL becomes **`https://an-lee.github.io/gh-wm/`** unless you use a custom domain — align `baseURL` accordingly.

---

## 7. Repository / remote rename (manual)

After code changes: rename the GitHub repository **`ghr` → `gh-wm`**, update local `git remote`, and fix any badges or external links.

---

## Architecture (high level)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  user["User"]
  gh["gh binary"]
  ext["gh-wm binary"]
  cfg["~/.gh-wm and GH_WM_CONFIG"]
  user -->|"gh wm ..."| gh
  gh -->|"exec + args"| ext
  ext --> cfg
```

---

## Risk / scope notes

- **Breaking change** for config path and env var; existing automation using `ghr` / `GHR_CONFIG` / `~/.ghr` must be updated or given a documented migration (or optional fallback).
- **GitHub Pages** and **go install** paths change with the module/repo rename; update any external bookmarks.

Root Use "wm"; rename config dir/env and GhrDir; update paths.go + templates + runner remote paths
Sweep user-facing ghr strings; fix docker/native tests and config tests
Makefile binary path; add gh-extension-precompile release workflow
Hugo config + docs + README for gh wm, install, and migration

input.description: Bulk-replace Go import paths ghr -> gh-wm
Move main.go from cmd/ghr to cmd/gh-wm

input.command:
cd /home/an-lee/projects/ghr && python3 << 'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path("cmd/gh-wm/main.go")
text = p.read_text()
text = text.replace("config.GhrDir()", "config.WmDir()")
text = text.replace('Use:   "ghr"', 'Use:   "wm"')
text = text.replace("~/.ghr", "~/.gh-wm")
text = text.replace("GHR_CONFIG", "GH_WM_CONFIG")
# User-facing command references
repls = [
    ("ghr setup", "gh wm setup"),
    ("ghr update", "gh wm update"),
    ("ghr:", "gh wm:"),
    ("SSH user, ghr ", "SSH user, gh wm "),
    ("(ghr service", "(gh wm service"),
    ("ghr up and ghr down", "gh wm up and gh wm down"),
    ("ghr service install", "gh wm service install"),
    ("`ghr`", "`gh wm`"),
    ("ghr manages", "GitHub Workflow Manager (gh wm) manages"),
    ("ghr opens", "gh wm opens"),
    ("ghr --help", "gh wm --help"),
    ("\"ghr config path\"", "\"gh wm config path\""),
    ("auto-detects everything else, and writes a working config ready for ghr up.", "auto-detects everything else, and writes a working config ready for gh wm up."),
    ("`ghr doctor` and `ghr status`", "`gh wm doctor` and `gh wm status`"),
    ("`ghr init --quick`", "`gh wm init --quick`"),
    ("=== ghr quick", "=== gh wm quick"),
    ("  ghr doctor", "  gh wm doctor"),
    ("  ghr up", "  gh wm up"),
    ("steps ghr cannot", "steps gh wm cannot"),
    ("Run `ghr init`", "Run `gh wm init`"),
    ("Remove autostart definitions installed by ghr", "Remove autostart definitions installed by gh wm"),
    ("created by ghr service", "created by gh wm service"),
    ("Reports whether ghr autostart", "Reports whether gh wm autostart"),
    ("Print ghr version", "Print gh wm version"),
    ("same remote install paths as ghr setup", "same remote install paths as gh wm setup"),
]
for a, b in repls:
    text = text.replace(a, b)
# Fix any double-replaced issues
text = text.replace("gh wm: stdout", "gh wm: stdout")  # no-op
# Remaining standalone "ghr" in strings - grep
p.write_text(text)
PY

input.query: GitHub Actions workflow for gh extension

input.contents:
# gh-wm — GitHub Workflow Manager (GitHub CLI extension)
# https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm

ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
BINARY := gh-wm.exe
else
BINARY := gh-wm
endif

CMD_DIR := ./cmd/gh-wm

GIT_TAG := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev)

PREFIX ?= /usr/local

.PHONY: all build test vet check clean install

all: build

build:
	go build -ldflags "-X main.version=$(GIT_TAG)" -o $(BINARY) $(CMD_DIR)/

test:
	go test ./... -race -count=1

vet:
	go vet ./...

check: vet test

clean:
	rm -f $(BINARY)

# Unix-like systems only (requires coreutils install); not for plain cmd.exe / PowerShell.
install: build
	install -d "$(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/bin"
	install -m 755 "$(BINARY)" "$(DESTDIR)$(PREFIX)/bin/$(BINARY)"

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: cli/gh-extension-precompile@v2
        with:
          go_version_file: go.mod

input.old_string: /ghr
input.new_string: /gh-wm

input.command:
cd /home/an-lee/projects/ghr && python3 << 'PY'
from pathlib import Path

def fix(text: str) -> str:
    text = text.replace("github.com/an-lee/ghr", "github.com/an-lee/gh-wm")
    text = text.replace("an-lee.github.io/ghr", "an-lee.github.io/gh-wm")
    text = text.replace("%USERPROFILE%\\.ghr", "%USERPROFILE%\\.gh-wm")
    text = text.replace("GHR_CONFIG", "GH_WM_CONFIG")
    text = text.replace("~/.ghr", "~/.gh-wm")
    text = text.replace(".ghr\\", ".gh-wm\\")
    text = text.replace(".ghr/", ".gh-wm/")
    text = text.replace("chmod 700 ~/.gh-wm", "chmod 700 ~/.gh-wm")  # no-op if already
    # preserve gh-aw
    text = text.replace("gh-aw", "\x00GHAW\x00")
    text = text.replace("ghr", "gh wm")
    text = text.replace("\x00GHAW\x00", "gh-aw")
    return text

for p in [Path("README.md"), Path("docs/hugo.toml")]:
    if p.exists():
        p.write_text(fix(p.read_text()))

for p in Path("docs/content").rglob("*.md"):
    p.write_text(fix(p.read_text()))
PY
input.description: Bulk-update docs and README paths and ghr→gh wm

input.old_string:
# gh wm

**gh wm** is a CLI for managing self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across many machines from one place—usually your laptop. It connects over **SSH** (or runs **locally** with `addr: local`), installs and starts runners, and gives you a **terminal UI** for day-to-day operations.
input.new_string:
# GitHub Workflow Manager (`gh wm`)

**GitHub Workflow Manager** is a [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) extension for managing self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across many machines from one place—usually your laptop. It connects over **SSH** (or runs **locally** with `addr: local`), installs and starts runners, and gives you a **terminal UI** for day-to-day operations.

input.old_string:
## Install

```bash
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-wm/cmd/gh wm@latest
```

Requires a recent **Go** toolchain (see `go.mod` / CI for the exact version).
input.new_string:
## Install

Install as a GitHub CLI extension (recommended):

```bash
gh extension install an-lee/gh-wm
```

Then use **`gh wm`** (for example `gh wm status`). Requires [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) (`gh`) and a recent `gh` version.

From source with Go:

```bash
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-wm/cmd/gh-wm@latest
```

The resulting binary is named `gh-wm`. To invoke it as **`gh wm`**, install the extension as above, or run `gh-wm` directly if it is on your `PATH`.

Requires a recent **Go** toolchain when building from source (see `go.mod` / CI for the exact version).

### Migrating from the old `ghr` CLI

If you previously used **`ghr`** with config under **`~/.ghr`** and **`GHR_CONFIG`**, move your data and update your environment:

```bash
mv ~/.ghr ~/.gh-wm
# If you used GHR_CONFIG, set GH_WM_CONFIG to the same path instead.
```

input.old_string:

## Install with Go

input.new_string:
# Installation

## Install as a GitHub CLI extension (recommended)

Requires [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) (`gh`).

```bash
gh extension install an-lee/gh-wm
```

After installing, you invoke the tool as **`gh wm`**. It does not create config files until you run:

## Install with Go

```bash
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-wm/cmd/gh-wm@latest
```

This installs a binary named **`gh-wm`** on your `PATH`. Use **`gh wm`** by installing the extension as above, or run `gh-wm` directly.

`go install` does not create config files. After installing, run:

input.old_string:
This repository is [an-lee/gh wm](https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm); the Go module path is `github.com/an-lee/gh-wm`.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm.git
cd gh wm
go build -o gh wm ./cmd/gh wm/
```

On Windows without GNU Make, use `go build` as above; Go writes `gh wm.exe` when you pass `-o gh wm`.
input.new_string:
This repository is [an-lee/gh-wm](https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm); the Go module path is `github.com/an-lee/gh-wm`.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm.git
cd gh-wm
go build -o gh-wm ./cmd/gh-wm/
```

On Windows without GNU Make, use `go build` as above; Go writes `gh-wm.exe` when you pass `-o gh-wm.exe`.

# gh wm

**gh wm** is a CLI tool to manage self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across multiple machines from a single control host—typically your laptop—using SSH or local execution.
input.new_string:

title: "GitHub Workflow Manager"
weight: 1

# GitHub Workflow Manager

**GitHub Workflow Manager** (`gh wm`) is a [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) extension to manage self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across multiple machines from a single control host—typically your laptop—using SSH or local execution.

input.old_string: The CLI reads your YAML config, connects to each host over SSH (or runs locally for `addr: local`), and runs the right commands to install, start, and stop runner processes. Only the machine where you run **gh wm** needs the `gh wm` binary.
input.new_string: The CLI reads your YAML config, connects to each host over SSH (or runs locally for `addr: local`), and runs the right commands to install, start, and stop runner processes. Only the machine where you run **gh wm** needs the GitHub CLI and this extension (or the `gh-wm` binary on `PATH`).

input.old_string:
```bash
go install github.com/an-lee/gh-wm/cmd/gh wm@latest
gh wm init
input.new_string:
```bash
gh extension install an-lee/gh-wm
gh wm init

input.old_string: `/etc/systemd/system/gh wm-runner-<instance>.service`
input.new_string: `/etc/systemd/system/ghwm-runner-<instance>.service`

todos.content: Makefile binary path; add gh-extension-precompile release workflow
Hugo config + docs + README for gh wm, install, and migration

input.command: cd /home/an-lee/projects/ghr && go vet ./... && go test ./... -race -count=1 -short 2>&1 | tail -15

content.text:
Summary of what was implemented:

### Module and binary
- **`go.mod`**: module is `github.com/an-lee/gh-wm`.
- **`cmd/gh-wm/main.go`**: root command `Use: "wm"`; imports and docs refer to **`gh wm`**; `config.WmDir()` replaces `GhrDir()`.
- **`cmd/ghr/`** removed (replaced by `cmd/gh-wm/`).

### Config and paths
- **`internal/config/paths.go`**: `~/.gh-wm`, **`GH_WM_CONFIG`**, **`WmDir()`**, same resolution order as before.
- Templates, **`internal/config/load.go`**, host runner dirs, autostart paths, systemd/launchd IDs, and Windows task names updated (`ghwm-runner-*`, `com.github.ghwm.runner.*`).
- Remote metrics markers: **`GH_WM_METRICS_*`** (replacing `GHR_METRICS_*`).
- Docker Windows isolated config: **`gh-wm-docker-config`**, **`ghwm.invalid`**, PowerShell vars **`$ghwmDockerConfigDir`**.

### Code references
- All **`github.com/an-lee/gh-wm`** imports; user-facing copy uses **`gh wm`**; **`gh-aw`** left unchanged.

### Build / release
- **`Makefile`**: builds **`gh-wm`** / **`gh-wm.exe`** from **`./cmd/gh-wm`**.
- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`**: **`cli/gh-extension-precompile@v2`** on tags `v*`, **`go_version_file: go.mod`**.
- **`.gitignore`**: ignore **`/gh-wm`** instead of **`/ghr`**.

### Docs and examples
- **`README.md`**: GitHub Workflow Manager positioning, **`gh extension install an-lee/gh-wm`**, **`go install …/cmd/gh-wm@latest`**, migration from **`ghr` / ~/.ghr / GHR_CONFIG**.
- **`docs/hugo.toml`**, **`docs/content/**`**, **`config/runners.yml`**: URLs, commands, paths, and file tree (`cmd/gh-wm`) aligned with the extension.

### Manual step for you
- Rename the GitHub repo to **`gh-wm`**, set **`git remote`** to `https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm.git`, and tag a release (**`v*`**) so **`gh extension install`** can use published binaries.

`go vet ./...` and **`go test ./... -race -count=1`** both succeed.
